ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a Turkish mixed seafood sharing platter. FOODON_03315763 (seafood dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Fish and seafood products) is the closest available category. No specific code exists for a mixed Turkish seafood combo platter.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for a mixed Turkish seafood sharing platter. SNOMED CT clinical food coding does not extend to composite restaurant platters of this type.

serving.natural_language_portion: A shared seafood platter for 2–4 persons, estimated at approximately 1,200 g total (300 g per person), comprising a mixed assortment of grilled fish, prawns, calamari, and mussels with accompaniments.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fish and seafood composite; Grilled mixed seafood reference values (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a Turkish mixed seafood combo platter. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition of USDA SR Legacy entries for grilled fish fillet (FDC ID 175168), cooked shrimp (FDC ID 175177), cooked calamari/squid (FDC ID 175181), and cooked mussels (FDC ID 175185), assuming approximately equal proportions by weight of the four main seafood components, with olive oil and lemon seasoning factored in. Values represent a per-100 g estimate of the combined edible seafood portion. Accompaniments (bread, rice) are excluded from the per-100 g calculation to represent the seafood component only.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — seafood and fish entries
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Turkish mixed seafood combo platter. GI is estimated at 25 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entries for grilled fish and mixed seafood dishes, which consistently fall in the low GI range (GI 0–30) due to negligible starch content and high protein content. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (25 × 38.4) / 100 ≈ 9.6, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 3.2 × 3 = 9.6 g; GL = (25 × 38.4) / 100 = 9.6 for the full 1200 g platter shared across 4 persons. Per-person GL ≈ 2.4 (low). Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021;114(5):1625-1632.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from seafood and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: A 300 g per-person serving provides approximately 960 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should request reduced salt seasoning and limit portion size to manage daily sodium intake below 2,000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and high protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 25 and only 9.6 g carbohydrate per 300 g serving, this mixed seafood platter has a low glycemic load and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-GI accompaniments such as white rice or pide bread.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Omega-3 fatty acids from mixed seafood and low saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of grilled fish, prawns, calamari, and mussels provides beneficial omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids and low saturated fat (approximately 1.2 g per 100 g), supporting cardiovascular health when prepared with olive oil rather than butter or heavy sauces.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Turkish Seafood Combo Platter (2–4 Persons) is a generously portioned sharing platter served at Turkish and Lebanese restaurants in Singapore, notably at Sultan Palace in Boat Quay. The platter typically comprises a mixed assortment of grilled fish fillets, whole or butterflied prawns, calamari rings or tubes, and mussels, all seasoned with classic Turkish aromatics including sumac, paprika, garlic, fresh parsley, dill, and a generous drizzle of olive oil, finished with lemon wedges. Preparation methods vary by component: fish fillets and prawns are typically grilled over charcoal or on a flat grill, calamari may be grilled or lightly pan-fried, and mussels are steamed or baked. The platter is served with accompaniments such as Turkish pide bread, rice pilaf, fresh salad, and mezze dips including hummus and cacık (yoghurt-cucumber dip). The flavour profile is clean, savoury, and herb-forward, with a light char from grilling and brightness from lemon and sumac. Texturally, the dish offers variety: flaky fish, firm prawns, tender calamari, and plump mussels. Nutritionally, the seafood component is high in lean protein (approximately 18.5 g per 100 g), rich in omega-3 fatty acids, vitamin B12, selenium, and phosphorus, and low in saturated fat. The glycemic index is low (approximately 25), making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is moderate and should be considered by those with hypertension. This dish is halal-certified at the serving restaurants and is dairy-free. It is priced at SGD 65 for the combo and is designed as a shared centrepiece for group dining.
common_user_queries: What is in a Turkish seafood platter?
Is Turkish seafood combo halal in Singapore?
Calories in Turkish mixed seafood platter
Is seafood combo good for diabetics?
Turkish restaurant seafood sharing platter Singapore
Seafood combo for 4 people Turkish cuisine

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a Turkish mixed seafood combo platter as served in Singapore. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for the individual seafood components (grilled fish, cooked shrimp, cooked calamari, cooked mussels) in approximately equal proportions. GI is estimated by analogy from University of Sydney GI Database seafood entries. Confidence is medium rather than low because the individual component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent the seafood component of the platter only; accompaniments (pide bread, rice pilaf, salad, mezze dips) are excluded from per-100 g calculations. Including accompaniments would increase carbohydrate_g, energy_kcal, and sodium_mg substantially. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy entries for grilled fish and seafood; naturally zero in unprocessed seafood prepared without hydrogenated fats. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (2.8), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.3), potassium_mg (310.0), calcium_mg (52.0), iron_mg (1.8), cholesterol_mg (95.0), vitamin_c_mg (2.5), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (28.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.08), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.12), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (3.8), vitamin_b6_mg (0.18), vitamin_b12_mcg (8.5), folate_mcg_dfe (18.0), vitamin_d_mcg (4.2), vitamin_e_mg (1.1), phosphorus_mg (220.0), magnesium_mg (38.0), zinc_mg (1.6), selenium_mcg (32.0), water_g (70.5) are all estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite of the four main seafood components. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record as all fields have defensible estimated values from USDA approved sources. 4. Serving size of 1,200 g total (300 g per person for 4 persons) is an estimate based on typical Turkish seafood platter portioning at restaurant level; actual weight will vary by restaurant. 5. Halal status is based on the restaurant context (Sultan Palace Turkish and Lebanese Cuisine); individual ingredient sourcing has not been independently verified. 6. Price of SGD 65.00 is preserved from the field survey data source record (GD000279). 7. GI of 25 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 8. The food_name has been normalised from the menu-style '58. Seafood Combo 2/4Pax' to the canonical English form 'Turkish Seafood Combo Platter (2–4 Persons)' per Veritas v2 Q1 requirements.
